text.title: Laravel 11 Architecture and Layer Responsibilities
text.summary: Project structure and layer responsibilities for Laravel 11 applications with Service/Action, FormRequest, and API Resource patterns.

rule.statement: Controllers stay thin: accept FormRequest, call one Action/Service, return API Resource. No validation logic, no DB queries, no event dispatching inline.
rule.rationale: Controllers that own business logic cannot be tested without booting the HTTP kernel. Service/Action classes are plain PHP and testable in isolation.

rule.statement: Every POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE endpoint must use a dedicated FormRequest class. FormRequest handles both authorization (authorize()) and validation (rules()).
rule.rationale: Inline $request->validate() in controllers duplicates authorization logic and scatters validation rules across the codebase.

rule.statement: Multi-step writes must be wrapped in DB::transaction(fn () => ...). Never rely on individual model saves to be consistent.
rule.rationale: Without a transaction, partial writes persist after any exception, leaving data in an inconsistent state.

rule.statement: Always declare $fillable on every Eloquent model. Never leave both $fillable and $guarded empty simultaneously.
rule.rationale: Empty $fillable + empty $guarded = mass assignment on every column, enabling IDOR via update endpoints.

list.item: Services/ or Actions/ — business workflow classes
Repositories/ — optional; only when multiple data sources or mock boundary needed
DTOs/ — readonly PHP 8.2 classes for inter-layer data transfer
Models/ — Eloquent models with $fillable, $casts, scopes, relationships

rule.statement: Use enum casts for status/role columns: protected $casts = ['status' => StatusEnum::class]. Never manage status as a raw string with manual checks.
rule.rationale: Enum casts enforce valid values at the PHP layer and enable IDE autocompletion. Manual string checks scatter across the codebase and drift.

rule.statement: Every FK in a migration must declare cascadeOnDelete() or restrictOnDelete() explicitly. Every down() method must fully reverse the up().
rule.rationale: Implicit FK behavior differs across databases. Non-reversible migrations cannot be rolled back safely.
